{{Quote|If we're destroyed—if we all die—the concepts and ideals of the New Order die with us.|Gilad Pellaeon|<ref name="Specter" />}}
The '''Imperial Remnant''', also referred to as the '''Imperial Remnants''', '''Pellaeon's Empire''', the '''true Empire''' or just the '''Remnant''', was an [[oligarchy|oligarchical]] [[Politics/Legends|faction]] formed from various fractured elements of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|ousted]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Among its people, it continued to be referred to officially as the '''Galactic Empire''' or simply the '''Empire''' like its previous incarnation. The Imperial Remnant was located in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] with its [[Planet/Legends|planetary]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] on [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]]. With the Empire [[Battle of Endor/Legends|defeat]], the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] was [[Declaration of a New Republic/Legends|formed]] while the Imperial Remnant began to resist the Republic and sought to regain control of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] under its leadership, leading to a renewal of conflict. Under [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]], the Remnant began to dramatically lose territory, which eventually forced it to negotiate a [[Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty|treaty]] for peaceful coexistence with the New Republic. In the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] that followed, it entered the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], and joined the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]]. However, during [[Second Galactic Civil War|another war]], the Remnant departed the Alliance. Soon after, [[Jagged Fel]] [[Imperial Election of 44 ABY|was elected]] as [[Head of State (Imperial Remnant)|Head of State]] during the transition into a [[Democracy/Legends|democracy]], however Fel would be returned to power and would go on to transform the Remnant into a [[Fel dynasty|dynastic]] [[Fel Empire|monarchy]].

After the fall of the Empire, [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Natasi Daala]] formed the "[[United Warlord Fleets]]" out of the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] and began a campaign against the New Republic. However she failed, and relinquished full command [[Fleet/Legends|fleets]] to Gilad Pellaeon, who after the campaign failed pulled fleets out of the Deep Core, and annexed the [[Pentastar Alignment]] and seized fortified [[Sector/Legends|sector]]s on the fringes of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] and established the [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Council of Moffs]] on a temporary headquarters on [[Orinda/Legends|Orinda]]. It maintained a strong military and limited venues of public expression, but did not [[Tarkin Doctrine/Legends|rule by terror and force]] and abolished both [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] and anti-[[Alien/Legends|alien]] sentiment. The Imperial Remnant was able to expand it's borders through a successful [[Orinda campaign|campaign]] in that year. However, Republic advances over the next seven years reduced the Remnant to just eight [[Sector/Legends|sectors]] and it's headquarters relocated to Bastion as a result. Here, Pellaeon realized that the war was lost and sued for peace with the New Republic, ending the Galactic Civil War in [[19 ABY]].

The Imperial Remnant then entered into a tense but peaceful relationship with the New Republic, while for the next six years, a number of unrepentant Imperials fled to the Remnant, taking a vast amount of wealth with them to parts unknown. When the Republic requested Remnant assistance in repelling an invasion by the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] [[Yuuzhan Vong]] in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the Moffs elected to instead sit the war out and fortify their own territory against the invaders. After [[Yuuzhan Vong empire|conquering much of the galaxy]] and [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|capturing]] the New Republic capital of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the Yuuzhan Vong [[Battle of Bastion|attacked]] Bastion in [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]] and forced an Imperial retreat. Pellaeon was finally able to convince the reluctant Moff Council to take the fight to the enemy, and under his direction, the Imperial Remnant joined the Galactic Federation of Free Alliances, the New Republic's successor state. [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]] saw the Yuuzhan Vong [[Battle of Yuuzhan'tar|defeated]] by the Galactic Alliance's combined fleets at Coruscant, and Pellaeon later retired.

As a member of the Galactic Alliance, the Remnant took part in the brief, but devastating [[Swarm War]] in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] along side the [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]] against [[the Colony]] during a period of great expansion by the [[Killik]]. [[41 ABY]] saw Pellaeon returned as the new Imperial leader when Galactic Alliance [[Chief of State (Galactic Alliance)|Chief of State]] Darth Caedus requested aid in the Second Galactic Civil War against a [[Confederation]] of breakaway [[Planet/Legends|planets]]. When Pellaeon refused to support Caedus' brutal tactics in the [[Second Battle of Fondor]], he was assassinated with [[Drikl Lecersen]] acting as their ''de facto'' leader, who authorized the creation of a [[nanovirus]] that targeted the [[Blood/Legends|bloodline]] of the [[Hapan Royal House|royal family]] of the [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]]. After the war, the Galactic Alliance confronted the Moffs with an ultimatum: face war crime trials, or install a young man named Jagged Fel as their new Head of State and force the outmatched Confederation to surrender.

The Imperial Remnant withdrew from the Galactic Alliance after the war, but in 43 ABY, Fel participated in attempts at [[Unification Summit|reunification]]. He survived an assassination attempt in [[44 ABY]] by a power-hungry Admiral Daala, who hoped to usurp control of the Remnant. Fel subsequently issued reforms and called a general election among the Remnant's citizens, and with his assistance, Admiral [[Vitor Reige]] defeated the popular Daala and became the new Head of State. Fel later returned to the post and used his "[[Victory Without War]]" initiative to spread Imperial influence throughout the galaxy while [[Imperial Mission|aiding]] poverty-stricken worlds, and the Remnant absorbed some of the Galactic Alliance's territory as Fel [[Fel dynasty|ascended]] as Emperor, whose reign reorganized the Remnant.

==History==
===The birth of the Remnant===
{{Quote|One Empire, one fleet—only this will guarantee us victory.|Admiral Daala|<ref name="Darksaber" />}}
[[File:SidEmp.png|thumb|right|350px|The Galactic Empire that once stood united under Palpatine fractured following his death.]]
In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the Galactic Empire suffered a crushing defeat at the Battle of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], their opponent in the Galactic Civil War. The [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]] destroyed the Empire's [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], and Emperor Palpatine, also known as the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Sidious, was [[Battle of Endor/Legends#Demise of the Sith|killed]] by his former [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], who previously served him as the Imperial enforcer Darth Vader.<ref name="TEA" /> The battle also saw the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of Lord Vader and the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy's]] [[Death Squadron/Legends|Death Squadron]], and the [[Battlecruiser/Legends|battlecruiser]] [[Pride of Tarlandia/Legends|''Pride of Tarlandia'']] destroyed. Admiral [[Horst Strage]], commander of the next communications [[Starship/Legends|ship]] in line—the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']]—was killed when an [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion blast]] overloaded his neural shunt, and command of the fleet was left in the hands of the ''Chimaera''{{'s}} [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] Gilad Pellaeon. Finding his forces in disarray, Pellaeon ordered a retreat to the planet [[Annaj/Legends|Annaj]].<ref name="Warfare" />

With the fleet regrouped, Pellaeon called a council of war and advocated that they return to Endor and crush the Rebels. When [[Adye Prittick]], the [[Military rank/Legends|ranking]] admiral, proved indecisive, Admiral [[Blitzer Harrsk]] took part of the fleet and retreated into the Deep Core until a clear chain of command could be established. As the Emperor had never provided a succession plan in the event of his death, other [[Navy/Legends|naval]] [[Imperial officer/Legends|officers]] followed Harrsk's example and fortified their holdings, declaring themselves independent warlords.<ref name="Warfare" /> [[Grand Vizier/Legends|Grand Vizier]] [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]] claimed the Imperial throne and, wary of Alliance strikes against key targets, fortified strategic systems and [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress worlds]], which put the Empire on the defensive despite their numerical and economic advantages over the Rebels.<ref name="TEA" /> Countless worlds flocked to the fold of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the successor state of the Alliance, after hearing news of the Emperor's death.<ref name="EC">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref>
[[File:AdmiralAlienAndCaptainMustache.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Grand Admiral Thrawn efforts to restore the Empire would pave the way for the establishment of the Imperial statelet in the Outer Rim under Gilad Pellaeon.]]
The New Republic consolidated their holdings and won worlds over through diplomacy before executing an aggressive [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|military]] push into the Core against an Empire that was crumbling from within due to the machinations of its own ambitious [[Politics/Legends|politicians]]. By [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], the Empire's territory had been reduced to a quarter of its former size,<ref name="EC" /> and the state was widely considered to be merely the "remnants of the Empire."<ref name="HTTESB" /><ref name="DESB">''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]''</ref><ref name="A Grand Admiral Returns">{{WEGCite|story=A Grand Admiral Returns|book=Heir to the Empire Sourcebook}}</ref><ref name="NEGTC" /> Campaigns [[Thrawn campaign|by]] Grand Admiral [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] and [[Operation Shadow Hand|by]] the [[Transfer essence|reborn]] Darth Sidious [[Dark Empire|reclaimed]] swaths of lost territory, but Palpatine's [[Battle of Onderon (Galactic Civil War)|final death]] saw the fleet again splinter into warlordism and [[Crimson Empire|Imperial territory]] reduced to a narrow band stretching from the Outer Rim to the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]].<ref name="EC" /> Despite the self-proclaimed Emperor [[Xandel Carivus]] in 11 ABY, its remnants consisted of squabbling warlord factions, standoffish fortress worlds, and Moffs and governors which did not acknowledge Carivus.<ref name="TEA" />

Later in 11 ABY, Admiral Natasi Daala's [[Maw fleet]] emerged from years of seclusion in [[Maw Cluster/Legends|the Maw]] and prosecuted an ultimately disastrous [[Daala campaign|military campaign]] against the New Republic.<ref name="EC" /> After limping into the Deep Core aboard her sole remaining ship, the ''[[Gorgon]]'', she declared herself a freelance [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassador]] and spent one year traveling to the various Deep Core warlords' fortress worlds espousing a message of unification. Unwilling to give up their power, the feuding warlords dismissed her ideas and continued their [[Imperial Civil War]] against each other. While she was at the [[Kalist VI/Legends|fortress]] of "Supreme Warlord" [[Blitzer Harrsk]] in 12 ABY, the [[Crimson Command|forces]] of "[[High Admiral]]" [[Treuten Teradoc]] [[Battle of Harrsk's fortress|attacked]], fleeing after destroying the [[Shockwave (Imperial-class)|flagship]] of [[Zero Command|Harrsk's fleet]]. Harrsk ordered her at [[Blaster/Legends|blasterpoint]] to lead a [[Battle of Teradoc's fortress|counterassault]], and in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] above [[Hakassi|Teradoc's own fortress]], Daala, fed up with the warlords' squabbling, fired an ion cannon from the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] of the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Firestorm (Imperial-class)|''Firestorm'']] at Harrsk's own [[Whirlwind (Imperial-class)|''Whirlwind'']] and ordered the fleet to stand down.<ref name="Darksaber" />

[[File:Harder_to_Breathe_TERC.jpg|thumb|right|350px|Admiral Daala gasses thirteen Imperial Warlords in a move to unite their holdings.]]
Pellaeon, now a [[Vice Admiral/Legends|Vice Admiral]] in Teradoc's fleet, found himself agreeing with Daala's assertions over an open [[Comlink/Legends|comm channel]] that the warlords needed to stop fighting amongst themselves and focus their efforts against the New Republic. He ordered Teradoc's defensive fleet to similarly stand down and took a shuttle from his [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[13X]]'' to join Daala on the bridge of the ''Firestorm''. The ship fled into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] along with the ''13X'' and one more of Pellaeon's [[Destroyer/Legends|Destroyers]] to a negotiating point in the dead of space, where Daala and Pellaeon agreed that a leader needed to be chosen from among the warlords, and proposed a detente council. Using both of their reputations as pull, Pellaeon convinced thirteen Deep Core warlords—including Harrsk, Teradoc, [[Sander Delvardus]],<ref name="Darksaber" /> and [[Malfkla Yzu]]<ref name="NEC" />—to convene at a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] called [[Tsoss Beacon/Legends|Tsoss Beacon]], where they met inside of a storeroom. Daala insisted that they unite their forces and choose a nominal leader, but when three [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]]' worth of arguing amounted to nothing, she donned a [[Breath mask/Legends|breath mask]]. Pellaeon quickly put on one of his own as the Admiral released a [[Nerve toxin/Legends|nerve gas]] into the room, ending both the discussion and the warlords' lives.<ref name="Darksaber" />

The fleets of the dead warlords pledged themselves to Daala, the architect of the [[Imperial Reunification]].<ref name="Darksaber" /> In a departure from Palpatine's [[Humanocentrism]], she welcomed [[Gender/Legends|women]] and non-Humans to actively participate in military service. Daala intended to relinquish control of the United Warlord Fleets after launching a devastating strike at the New Republic; her target was the jungle [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], [[Jedi Praxeum|home]] to the New Republic–allied [[New Jedi Order]].<ref name="EC" /> However, the [[Force power/Legends|Force powers]] of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] students were able to [[Telekinesis/Legends|blast]] Pellaeon's vanguard fleet of seventeen Star Destroyers out of orbit,<ref name="Darksaber" /> and Daala's [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] ''[[Knight Hammer]]'' was disabled following an infiltration by the Jedi student [[Callista Ming]]. The [[Assault on Yavin 4]] was a disaster for the Imperials, and with her plans having fallen to rubble, Daala relinquished full command of the United Warlord Fleets to Pellaeon.<ref name="EC" />

Although not the type to seize power, Pellaeon proved himself an extremely capable leader.<ref name="EC" /> He strengthened ties with the Moffs of surviving Imperial factions in the Outer and Mid Rims—who had earlier supported Daala's United Warlord Fleets with with credits and war matériel—and decided to withdraw all of his fleets and war arsenals from the Deep Core, which he felt would keep his new statelet bottled up.<ref name="TEA" /> Before leaving, he discovered [[Squadron/Legends|squadrons]] of robotic [[TIE series/Legends|TIEs]], caches of arms, [[Mine/Legends|mines]], and the Star Dreadnoughts ''[[Megador]]'' and [[Dominion (Star Dreadnought)|''Dominion'']] within abandoned Deep Core armories.<ref name="Warfare" /> As the Outer and Mid Rims offered more resources, better-traveled [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]], and favorable opportunities to harass New Republic shipping,<ref name="EC" /> Pellaeon pledged his fleets to protect the Imperial Rim factions on the condition that they merged their territories under him.<ref name="TEA" /> The Moffs of the Rim factions,<ref name="Warfare" /> including the Pentastar Alignment,<ref name="TEA" /> agreed that the ''Megador'', ''Dominion'', and the [[Reaper (Executor-class)|''Reaper'']]—a dreadnaught belonging to the Pentastar Alignment—were enough to defend against the New Republic, and named Pellaeon the Supreme Commander<ref name="Warfare" /> of the new Imperial Remnant.<ref name="TEA" /> Donated to the statelet were their carefully-hoarded stores of munitions and war matériel.<ref name="EC" />

The borders of the Remnant were initially well-defined, stretching from the Mid Rim to Wild Space, with additional scattered holdings in other regions of the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]].<ref name="EC" /> These holdings included systems in the former [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Borderland Regions]] that acknowledged the rule of the planet Sartinaynian, which was defiantly renamed "Bastion." Pellaeon referred to his new state as the "true Empire,"<ref name="TEA" /> but it was also known as simply the "Empire."<ref name="Specter" /> The Remnant was also called "Pellaeon's Empire" in order to distinguish it from surviving Warlord factions,<ref name="NEC" /> such as the [[Constitutional Protectorate of Prakith]] and several Warlords who remained in the Deep Core.<ref name="TEA" />

===Expansion through conquest===
====Resumption of fighting====
{{Quote|Thanks to Daala's unification efforts, the Empire was stable, though still only a shadow of its former self. If anyone could restore the Empire… Pellaeon appeared to be the one.|[[Voren Na'al/Legends|Voren Na'al]]|<ref name="EC" />}}
Pellaeon knew that the Remnant was not capable of mounting a serious offensive against the galaxy's inner systems,<ref name="TEA" /> but he believed that the Outer and Mid Rims contained hundreds of thousands of worlds that were ripe for conquering.<ref name="EC" /> Both he and the Moffs were aware that some factions in the New Republic would prefer to see the Imperial Remnant wiped out completely; indeed, New Republic pickets were routinely pushing deep into the Northern Quadrant. As such, Pellaeon decided to show his enemies that the New Order was not yet dead by defending what he saw as the Remnant's borders.<ref name="TEA" /> While the fleet was preparing to mount an offensive against the New Republic, Imperial agents took over the [[New Republic embassy (Dathomir)|New Republic embassy]] on the planet [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] in order to investigate the ancient [[Star Temple]] and determine if the [[Infinity Wave]] [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] could be used in the war. After finding that there was nothing left of the [[Infinity Gate]]'s control chamber, [[General/Legends|General]] [[Vit]] attempted to salvage the mission by training the local [[Nightsisters/Legends|Nightsisters]] as [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-using Imperial [[Troop/Legends|troops]].<ref name="Nightsaber">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20031120nightsaber|text=Nightsaber|int=Nightsaber}}</ref>

{{RPG}}
Around the same time, fallen Jedi [[Dal Konur]] was on a one-man mission to [[Assassin/Legends|assassinate]] the members of the Moff Council. After encountering and escaping from the Imperial fleet, which was gathering near [[Agamar/Legends|Agamar]], he landed on Dathomir and was recruited to serve as a trainer in General Vit's "[[Project Nightsaber]]." However, Konur and Vit were defeated by a team of Jedi-allied heroes and a former [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Emperor's Royal Guard]], [[Kir Kanos]], who had come to Dathomir in search of Imperial scientist [[Sigit Ranth]]. Dathomir was accordingly liberated from the Remnant.<ref name="Nightsaber" />
{{Endgame}}

[[File:LusankyavsReaper-EGW.jpg|thumb|left|325px|The ''Reaper'' and the ''Lusankya'' square off over Orinda.]]
Six [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after Daala's resignation,<ref name="EC" /> Pellaeon took the ''Reaper'' to the planet [[Borosk/Legends|Borosk]] and retrieved a complement of fighters from the legendary [[181st Imperial Fighter Wing/Legends|181st Imperial Fighter Group]], which was led by starfighter [[Ace/Legends|ace]] [[Turr Phennir]]. Pellaeon and Phennir kicked off the [[Orinda campaign]] by swiftly [[Raid on Orinda|capturing]] Orinda and six systems coreward of it along the [[Entralla Route/Legends|Entralla Route]], [[Battle of Lonnaw|taking]] [[Lonnaw]] last. The Supreme Commander believed that holding Orinda would sufficiently demonstrate Imperial power without provoking an aggressive response, and as such, he dispatched emissaries to [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]] who stated that no further offensives would be launched if the Remnant's new borders were respected.<ref name="Warfare" />

The New Republic responded with force, as they feared another assault on the Core, and dispatched General [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] to the front lines aboard the ''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought ''[[Lusankya/Legends|Lusankya]]'', accompanied by the famous [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] of [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighters]]. Before Antilles's fleet arrived, Pellaeon [[Battle of Corthenia|took]] [[Battle of Urce|the]] [[Corthenia system/Legends|Corthenia]] and [[Urce system/Legends|Urce systems]] while Phennir, accompanied by a complement of [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]], [[Battle of Hewett|captured]] [[Battle of Tartaglia|the]] defenseless systems of [[Hewett system/Legends|Hewett]] and [[Tartaglia system/Legends|Tartaglia]] The arrival of the ''Lusankya'' at the front sparked a series of strikes and parries between the 181st and the Rogues as the ''Reaper'' and the ''Lusankya'' stalked each other throughout the Mid Rim; the first engagements came in the [[Darkon system/Legends|Darkon]] and [[Traval-Pacor system/Legends|Traval-Pacor systems]], and [[Battle of Darkon|both]] [[Battle of the Traval-Pacor system|battles]] were won by the New Republic, who secured the systems.<ref name="Warfare" />

The 181st were able to best the ''Lusankya'' in a [[Battle of Tyan|bid]] for control of the [[Vvan system|Tyan system]] after [[Battle of Orocco|battling]] the Rogues to a stalemate at [[Orocco system/Legends|Orocco]], and citizens of both the Remnant and the New Republic became enthralled by tales of the duels between the great capital ships of the opposing fleets. A less captivated New Republic military sent Admiral [[Areta Bell]] and the [[Endurance-class fleet carrier|fleet carrier]] [[Endurance (Endurance-class)|''Endurance'']] to back Antilles up, and Pellaeon gradually fell back. Antilles and Bell [[Battle of Obredaan|defeated]] the ''Reaper'' and the 181st in the [[Obredaan system/Legends|Obredaan system]], and a [[Battle of Orinda|final showdown]] came at Orinda, where Pellaeon sprang a trap. Six [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|interdictor cruisers]] and the ''Dominion''—whose existence had been kept a secret—decanted from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and destroyed the ''Endurance'' along with the squadrons of [[E-7 E-wing multi-role starfighter|E-wing fighters]] it held. The ''Lusankya'' fled with the Rogues, and the Remnant's southern borders were secured.<ref name="Warfare" /> The New Republic fortified surrounding systems, however,<ref name="EC" /> and border clashes became routine. Both governments maneuvered for position with neutral planets such as [[Adumar/Legends|Adumar]].<ref name="Warfare" />

Also in 12 ABY, a New Republic investigation into possible Imperial Remnant activity on the planet [[Kejim]]<ref name="Jedi Outcast">''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]''</ref> revealed a sinister plot by the Imperial splinter faction known as the [[Empire Reborn]].<ref name="The Dark Forces Saga Part 6">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050616a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 6: Outcasts and Megalomaniacs Welcome}}</ref>

====Force and counterforce====
{{Quote|I hope your father is not having ''second thoughts… ?"<br />"''About ''rejoining'' the Empire? No. No "second thoughts," but he ''has'' been thinking. He is concerned that the Empire is at a serious crossroads. This protracted war is draining the treasuries of the Empire and its allies. We cannot afford to continue on our present course.|Pellaeon and Feena D'Asta in 13 ABY|<ref name="Crimson Empire III 2">[[Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost 2|''Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost'' 2]]</ref>}}
[[File:Pellaeon_Empire_map.jpg|thumb|right|350px|Imperial space, following the Orinda campaign]]
The Wild Space planet Adumar, rich with ordnance-producing resources, had recently discovered the wider galaxy and was interested in joining either the Remnant or the New Republic. As the Adumari held fighter pilots in high esteem, Phennir and three other members of the 181st were invited to the planet in [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]] as diplomatic ambassadors, as were Wedge Antilles and three members of Rogue Squadron. Admiral [[Teren Rogriss]] ferried the 181st pilots to Adumar aboard the Star Destroyer [[Agonizer (starship)|''Agonizer'']] and gave his word to a representative of the planet's nation-state of [[Cartann]]: if Adumar ultimately joined the New Republic, the ''Agonizer'' would leave the [[Adumar system/Legends|system]] within the hour. However, Rogriss's instructions from on high were to disregard the order and return with an invasion force. The Admiral accordingly underwent a deep internal struggle regarding the question of honor.<ref name="Adumar">''[[X-Wing: Starfighters of Adumar]]''</ref>

Phennir and his men flew for Cartann when it declared [[Adumari Civil War|war]] on Adumar's other nation-states, but only Phennir survived Cartann's defeat to the Antilles-led [[Adumari Union]].After being offered New Republic clemency by Antilles if he decided to keep the word he had given, Rogriss defected from the Remnant and locked down the ''Agonizer''{{'s}} [[Holocomm/Legends|holocomm]] unit, thereby preventing the Imperials from alerting their superiors until they returned to Imperial space. A Remnant task force did return to Adumar and [[Battle of Adumar|attack]], but strong resistance from New Republic and Adumari forces forced a retreat.<ref name="Adumar" /> Pellaeon did not give up the conquest and initiated a protracted campaign against Adumar,<ref name="EC" /> [[Campaign against Adumar|bombarding]] the planet from the bridge of the ''Reaper''.<ref name="NEGTC" />

13 ABY also saw Moff [[Tol Getelles]] of the [[Antemeridian sector/Legends|Antemeridian sector]] conspire with the former [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]] [[Senator/Legends|senator]] [[Seti Ashgad/Legends|Seti Ashgad]] to take over the [[Meridian sector/Legends|Meridian sector]]. The Meridian sector lay on the contentious Imperial–New Republic border, and the neighboring Antemeridian was one of the various pockets of the Remnant that was still under the sway of petty governors and greedy Moffs.<ref name="EC" /> Getelles, still considered a warlord,<ref name="Warfare" /> was resentful of his new reality of having to pay fealty to Pellaeon, and his plan would see Ashgad cripple the Meridian sector by unleashing the [[Death Seed]] plague, after which Getelles's Sector Fleet would invade with the aid of [[needle fighter]]s secured from the [[Loronar Corporation/Legends|Loronar Corporation]]. However, Getelles's fleet was [[Battle of Nam Chorios|defeated]] by a makeshift New Republic force that had been hastily assembled by the Republic-allied [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]]<ref name="EC" /> concurrently with the Adumar mission.<ref name="Adumar" />

Solo and Calrissian were backed up by a fleet of the [[Independent Company of Settlers]], which was led by a retired Admiral Daala, who was disgusted with Getelles's actions and fought against him in the name of Imperial honor. Not long afterward, the New Republic pressed their advantage by bringing two fleets into the Antemeridian sector, where they [[Invasion of the Antemeridian sector|defeated]] Getelles and claimed both the sector and a large chunk of neighboring space.<ref name="EC" /> After the New Republic pushed through the Borderlands and toward the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]] and Imperial territory, the Council of Moffs—fearing a power grab<ref name="TEA" /> and emboldened by Pellaeon's victory in the Orinda campaign—ordered the Supreme Commander,<ref name="Warfare" /> who was preoccupied at Adumar,<ref name="EC" /> to go on the offensive<ref name="Warfare" /> and send his forces across the Hydian Way.<ref name="TEA" />

Adumar ultimately remained a New Republic world<ref name="Crimson Empire III 2" /> as a reluctant Pellaeon<ref name="Warfare" /> launched Remnant fleets from Imperial space and attacked the New Republic on two fronts, [[Battle of Barison|scoring]] [[Battle of Lucazec|victories]] at both [[Barison]] and [[Lucazec/Legends|Lucazec]]. Grievous losses were inflicted against the Remnant's enemies, but the [[Battle of Arda|next]] [[Battle of Quell (Galactic Civil War)|battles]], fought at [[Arda system/Legends|Arda]] and [[Quell/Legends|Quell]], were both won by the New Republic. Pellaeon was forced back to [[Celanon/Legends|Celanon]],<ref name="TEA" /> where he was [[Battle of Celanon|soundly defeated]] by the New Republic's Admiral [[Etahn A'baht]], losing the ''Reaper'' in the process.<ref name="Warfare" /> Rather than push their advantage, the New Republic chose to cool the fires of war by jump-starting commerce,<ref name="TEA" /> while Pellaeon attempted to build up the Remnant's defenses and turn it into a functioning state that could co-exist with the New Republic.<ref name="Warfare" /> At some point during the year, the subterranean research-and-development [[Laboratory/Legends|laboratories]] of an [[Imperial Navy base]] on the planet [[Ryvester]] were [[Mission to Ryvester|destroyed]] by members of the New Republic's [[Wraith Squadron]], after the Imperial Admiral [[Kosh Teradoc]] inadvertently smuggled a bomb of theirs inside.<ref name="Mercy Kill">''[[X-Wing: Mercy Kill]]''</ref>

[[File:Sodietraitors.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Admiral Pellaeon is apprehended by stormtroopers of the Restored Empire.]]
Before the year came to an end, Pellaeon was made aware that [[Baron of the Empire/Legends|Baron]] [[Ragez D'Asta]]<ref name="Crimson Empire III 2" />—a powerful shipping magnate whose [[D'Asta private fleet|private fleet]] had once served the Galactic Empire<ref name="Crimson Empire Handbook D'Asta">[[Star Wars Handbook 2: Crimson Empire|''Star Wars Handbook'' 2: ''Crimson Empire'']] {{C|under "Baron Ragez D'Asta"}}</ref>—was considering joining the Remnant. The [[Baron/Legends|Baron's]] daughter, [[Feena D'Asta|Feena]], visited Pellaeon on Orinda and revealed that her [[Parent/Legends|father]] was concerned about the drain on the Remnant's resources that the ongoing war was causing. If Pellaeon were to seek a truce with the New Republic, the Baron would intercede with the Moffs and give the Supreme Commander his full backing.<ref name="Crimson Empire III 2" /> While Pellaeon considered her proposal, Imperial forces were [[Battle of Pallaxides|defeated]] by a New Republic fleet at [[Pallaxides]].<ref name="Crimson Empire III 3">[[Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost 3|''Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost'' 3]]</ref> Feena D'Asta soon after represented the Remnant at peace negotiations with [[Mirith Sinn]], a representative of the New Republic, but the talks were [[Ambush at the peace negotiations|ambushed]] by stormtroopers of the [[Restored Empire]],<ref name="Crimson Empire III 5" /> an Imperial splinter faction whose leadership hoped to take down both the Remnant and the New Republic.<ref name="Crimson Empire III 3" /> Feena D'Asta was killed, and Pellaeon—believing the New Republic was responsible—ordered an immediate retaliatory strike at [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the New Republic's capital, in order to demonstrate solidarity to the [[D'Astan sector/Legends|D'Astan sector]].<ref name="Crimson Empire III 5" />

Thanks to a tip from Kir Kanos,<ref name="Despoilers 2">{{SW|url=news/the-imperial-warlords-despoilers-of-an-empire-part-2|series=The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire|text=Part 2|nolive=1}}</ref> the Supreme Commander made a contingency plan and arranged for part of D'Asta's fleet to hide behind [[Orinda's moon]].<ref name="Crimson Empire III 6">[[Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost 6|''Crimson Empire III: Empire Lost'' 6]]</ref> While the Orinda fleet was en route to Coruscant, the nearly defenseless Imperial capital was [[Battle of Orinda (13 ABY)|attacked]] by the [[Venator-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Venator''-class Star Destroyers]] of the Restored Empire,<ref name="Crimson Empire III 5" /> and when the faction's stormtroopers landed on-world and blasted their way right to Pellaeon, the Supreme Commander was confronted by the Restored Empire's leader, [[Ennix Devian]]. Pellaeon shot and killed his dignitary [[Zam Basdor]] after learning that the man had been secretly reporting to Devian, and was then given an ultimatum: join Devian and restore the Empire, or die. Pellaeon instead revealed that Devian's Venators were even then being destroyed by the D'Asta fleet, and the Baron himself arrived on the scene with his own stormtrooper complement. The resulting skirmish saw Devian killed in combat with Kir Kanos, and the Restored Empire was defeated.<ref name="Crimson Empire III 6" />

====Losing ground====
{{Quote|A lot of time has passed since the last of the Imperials were beaten back, and we've had a good look through the Imperial archives. Virtually all Imperial forces have been accounted for. You might be able to spin out a story where someone managed to scrape together a task force from ships mistakenly listed as destroyed. Some people say there are whole fleets out there that no one knows about. But even if that were true, where are the thousands of trained crew supposed to come from? Every time anything goes wrong anywhere in the Republic, some conspiracy buff or another trots out a theory of a cabal intent on reviving the Empire. If someone runs out of place mats in the palace commissary, it's an Imperial plot. I for one no longer believe in that particular bogeyman. The Empire is as dead as Darth Vader.|Han Solo, to [[Dracmus]], in 18 ABY|<ref name="AaS">''[[Assault at Selonia]]''</ref>}}
[[File:MonCals_vs_ImpStar-Korriban.jpg|thumb|right|230px|New Republic forces attack an ''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer in orbit during the Battle of Korriban.]]
In [[14 ABY/Legends|14 ABY]], the Remnant cooperated with a [[Sith cult/Legends|Sith cult]] known as the [[Disciples of Ragnos]], who sought to resurrect the spirit of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Marka Ragnos]] and rebuild a new [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]]. During the [[Disciples of Ragnos crisis]], the Remnant was active at locations such as an [[Bakuran power station|old power station]] on [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]], [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], [[Bast Castle]] on [[Vjun/Legends|Vjun]], [[Kril'Dor/Legends|Kril'Dor]], [[Dosuun]], [[Yalara]], the remains of [[Byss/Legends|Byss]], and [[Taspir III]]. The Remnant had several run-ins with members of the New Jedi Order, and the Master-and-apprentice pair of [[Kyle Katarn]] and [[Jaden Korr]] proved to be thorns in their side, thwarting Remnant operations time and again. For instance, the Imperial Army officer [[Rax Joris]] was killed by Korr on Dosuun. Under the command of the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Tavion Axmis]], Remnant troops fought alongside [[Sith cultist/Legends|Sith Cultists]] and other [[New Reborn]] Dark Jedi, as well as [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] hired by the Disciples of Ragnos. A few Remnant troops were present at the [[Battle of Korriban (Disciples of Ragnos crisis)|Battle of Korriban]], but were not involved in the fighting until they were attacked by Jaden Korr who single-handedly managed to eliminate them all and move on to defeat Axmis, thus ending the cooperation between the Remnant and the Sith Cult for good.<ref name="Jedi Academy" />

Over the next several years, the Remnant continued to wilt, but Pellaeon scored a personal victory when he reclaimed the ''Chimaera'', his old flagship, from the New Republic's Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]] at the [[Battle of Gravlex Med]].<ref name="NEGTC" /> In the year [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]], two Star Destroyers of the long-lost [[Black Fleet]] emerged from the [[Koornacht Cluster/Legends|Koornacht Cluster]] and joined the Remnant, as did the experimental weapons test bed [[Rakehell (Sorannan-class)|''EF-X'']].<ref name="EC" /> During this largely peaceful period, Imperial systems near the Hydian Way began to push for an end to the hostilities between the Imperial Remnant and the New Republic, an offer made all the more enticing in [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]] when<ref name="TEA" /> the New Republic Senate made a [[New Republic amnesty bill|peace offering]] by allowing former Imperials to hold elected office. The Council of Moffs believed it to be a prelude to invasion<ref name="Warfare" /> and secretly contacted Admiral Daala, who had united a group of [[replacement warlord]]s in the Deep Core. They proposed that she aid the Remnant in a decisive campaign against the New Republic,<ref name="TEA" /> and when she obliged, they similarly ordered Pellaeon into battle.<ref name="Warfare" /> Although he believed the plan to be a mad one, he acquiesced to the council's wishes, hoping that a handful of victories would allow him to convince the Moffs to seek a peace treaty.<ref name="TEA" /> The Remnant thus launched their [[Imperial Skirmishes|campaign]] to reclaim formerly-held sectors, believing that the New Republic would decline to respond in full force out of a fear of creating a public relations debacle.<ref name="EC" />

After scoring a [[Battle of Adumar (17 ABY)|victory]] at Adumar, Pellaeon launched [[First battle of Taris|surprise]] [[Battle of Champala|strikes]] at [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] and [[Champala/Legends|Champala]], the latter an [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] world. Both battles were Imperial victories; concurrently, Daala was handily [[Battle of Columus (Galactic Civil War)|defeated]] at [[Columus/Legends|Columus]] by the [[New Republic Fourth Fleet]] under General [[Garm Bel Iblis/Legends|Garm Bel Iblis]]. With Daala out of the war,<ref name="TEA" /> Bel Iblis's fourth fleet, as well as the [[New Republic Third Fleet|Third]] and [[New Republic Fifth Fleet|fifth fleets]] under Admiral Ackbar, routed Pellaeon's forces at [[Second Battle of Champala|Champala]],<ref name="Warfare" /> [[Second battle of Taris|Taris]],<ref name="TEA" /> [[Battle of Ketaris (17 ABY)|Ketaris]], and [[Battle of Tangrene (17 ABY)|Tangrene]]. The [[Battle of Anx Minor|final engagement]] came at [[Anx Minor/Legends|Anx Minor]], where Pellaeon deployed the ''Dominion'', ''Megador'', and the ''EF-X''—renamed the ''Rakehell''—hoping for a repeat of Orinda. However, [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]] knew of a weak point in the ''Rakehell''{{'s}} [[Armor/Legends|armor]], against which Ackbar ordered multiple strikes. The ship's [[antimatter]] reservoir exploded,<ref name="Warfare" /> destroying six Star Destroyers,<ref name="NEC" /> damaging the ''Megador'', and prompting Pellaeon to order a retreat to Bastion.<ref name="Warfare" /> The Remnant's navy was decimated,<ref name="NEGTC" /> and it found itself controlling a mere eight sectors in a strategically unimportant area of the Outer Rim<ref name="EC" /> with a single major [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]].<ref name="Specter" /> Pellaeon told the eight remaining Moffs that he was finished obeying their orders; as five years of work had been undone in a matter of weeks, they accepted his proclamation.<ref name="Warfare" /> [[18 ABY/Legends|One year later]], when the [[Corellian Crisis]] erupted, the New Republic kept a tight lid on the Remnant throughout the duration of the conflict.<ref name="TEA" />

====Peace at last====
{{Quote|Tell them that it's time to send an emissary to the New Republic. To discuss the terms of our surrender.|Pellaeon, to Captain Ardiff, in 19 ABY|<ref name="Specter" />}}
In [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]], the failure of new [[Computerized Combat Predictor]] technology to live up to expectations was the last straw for Pellaeon, and he finally accepted that the war was over—the Imperial Remnant, with its limited territory and inferior fleet, had lost. He was able to convince the Council of Moffs that a negotiated treaty with the New Republic would allow them to keep their territory rather than lose what little they had left, but an attempt at thwarting his plans was quickly set into motion by a [[Sinister Triumvirate]] of Imperials: Moff [[Vilim Disra]] of the [[Braxant sector/Legends|Braxant sector]], former Emperor's Royal Guard [[Grodin Tierce (clone)|Grodin Tierce]], and the [[Con artist/Legends|con artist]] [[Flim (Human)|Flim]], who was posing as a reborn Grand Admiral Thrawn. Several Star Destroyer captains in Disra's [[Braxant Sector Fleet|sector fleet]] pledged their loyalty to the man they believed to be a reborn Thrawn, and they were dispatched on a number of missions whose purposes they did not understand, including the [[Mission to Morishim|interception]] of Pellaeon's peace courier to the New Republic at [[Morishim]].<ref name="Specter" />

[[File:HandofThrawnEC.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The Sinister Triumvirate of Moff Disra, Major Grodin Tierce, and the con artist Flim attempted to seize control of the Remnant in 19 ABY.]]
While Pellaeon was touring Imperial space in order to present his peace plan to other senior officials, the New Republic remained unaware of his entreaty, all the while dealing with a major [[Caamas Document Crisis|internal crisis]] regarding the recent discovery of a partial copy of the [[Caamas Document]]. The document revealed that several [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]] were responsible for the [[19 BBY/Legends|long-ago]] [[Caamas Firestorm]], and agents loyal to Disra worked to fuel the fires brewing within New Republic member worlds whose citizens were calling for retribution against the entire Bothan species. The Triumvirate's goal was to bring about the New Republic's collapse from within, thus allowing them to take over the galaxy after seizing power within the Remnant. "Thrawn" made himself known to the New Republic, offering to personally sit down with the [[Clan|Bothan clan leaders]] and discern specifically whom was responsible for the Caamas Firestorm, and his offer served the Triumvirate's goals by prompting a large amount of political infighting in the New Republic Senate.<ref name="Specter" />

When Pellaeon arrived in the [[Pesitiin system/Legends|Pesitiin system]], where he hoped to meet with New Republic General Garm Bel Iblis, the ''Chimaera'' was attacked by the Disra-loyal [[Cavrilhu Pirates]], who were posing as [[Corellian Defense Force/Legends|Corellian Defense Force]] ships loyal to Bel Iblis. They were handily defeated by Pellaeon, who did not fall for the ruse and who decided to continue waiting for Bel Iblis to arrive.<ref name="Specter" /> Not even word of Thrawn's return could sway him from his course. When the New Republic [[Slicer/Legends|slicer]] [[Zakarisz Ghent]] was able to decrypt the garbled recording of Pellaeon's peace offer that [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Meizh Vermel]] had attempted to transmit at Morishim before being captured, Councilor [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] rushed to Pesitiin and met with Pellaeon. Preliminary peace discussions were conducted aboard her starship, the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], and Pellaeon offered the Empire's complete copy of the Caamas Document—which included the names of the specific Bothans responsible—in exchange for favorable peace terms. He thus arranged for Ghent to slice into the Ubiqtorate station at Yaga Minor to retrieve a complete copy from the Emperor's [[Special Files]]—documents to which even the Supreme Commander did not have access.<ref name="Vision">''[[Vision of the Future]]''</ref>

Meanwhile, the Triumvirate ordered Imperial Captain [[Nalgol]] to take the [[Cloaking device/Legends|cloaked]] Star Destroyers ''[[Tyrannic]]'', ''[[Obliterator]]'', and [[Ironhand (Imperial-class)|''Ironhand'']] to the [[Both system]]<ref name="Specter" /> and wait for the nearby New Republic warships—half of which supported the Bothans and half of which called for their punishment—to come to blows. Orders from "Thrawn" were sent out to [[sleeper cell]]s of [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] that the former Grand Admiral had placed throughout the galaxy ten years prior, and the Triumvirate attempted to learn more about what Thrawn's greater goals had been by investigating secret mentions of something called the "Hand of Thrawn." Imperial territory was bolstered when the planet [[Kroctar]] requested re-admission into the Remnant, and when Flim, with the help of Tierce's tactical genius, was able to help members of the [[Ruurian/Legends|Ruurian]] species ward off an attack from their [[Diamal]]a rivals. The move prompted the grateful Ruurians to pledge both the [[Ruuria system/Legends|Ruuria system]] and the [[Unified Ruurian Colonies]] to the Remnant. Around the same time, the Triumvirate cut off their ties with the Cavrilhu Pirates and began to pursue a partnership with the deadly [[Mistryl Shadow Guard]]s.<ref name="Vision" /> 19 ABY also saw the Remnant's [[Mulvar Sensor Station]] in the Outer Rim destroyed by Wraith Squadron.<ref name="Mercy Kill" />

Pellaeon returned to Bastion to confront Disra, who (he had learned) had recently accessed the Special Files at Yaga Minor. Although Disra denied the accusation, he left his office to attend to an urgent matter, and Pellaeon and Commander [[Dreyf]] found [[Datacard/Legends|datacards]] in his desk that contained incriminating evidence. They also learned that Colonel Vermel had been kidnapped by Disra's agents and imprisoned on Yaga Minor, and they immediately left to retrieve him. Disra's urgent matter was the New Republic's Han Solo and Lando Calrissian, who were on Bastion, attempting to steal a copy of the Caamas Document. "Thrawn" appeared before them and gave them a datacard, claiming that it contained the information they sought; he also asserted that recent riots on [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]] had actually been instigated by the New Republic's Senator [[Borsk Fey'lya]]. The lies shook up Solo and Calrissian, who left with their prize, unaware that it was in fact an altered copy.<ref name="Vision" />

[[File:Pellaeon-Gavrisom_Treaty1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Pellaeon and the New Republic's President Gavrisom sign peace accords, bringing the Galactic Civil War to an end.]]
When Pellaeon returned to Bastion, he learned that Disra and Thrawn had gone to Yaga Minor, but before he could follow, an unknown alien ship appeared in space and hailed him, offering him something that it claimed he desperately wanted. At the same time, Disra's agent [[Drend Navett]] was able to sabotage Bothawui's [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shield]], and his allies infiltrated the [[Ishori]] war [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] ''[[Predominance]]'' and fired on the planet. The Triumvirate's plans for the New Republic came to fruition as a large [[Battle of Bothawui (Galactic Civil War)|battle]] erupted among the New Republic vessels, threatening to start an all-out civil war. However, in the midst of the chaos, Han Solo spotted Captain Nalgol's cloaked Star Destroyers and the New Republic forces united against their common enemy.<ref name="Vision" />

General Bel Iblis led a concurrent New Republic [[Battle of Yaga Minor (Galactic Civil War)|strike at Yaga Minor]] in an attempt to retrieve the Caamas Document, but the Triumvirate had expected his attack and caught the General's [[Errant Venture/Legends|flagship]] in a [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]]. However, Pellaeon arrived at Yaga Minor and confronted the Triumvirate aboard the Star Destroyer [[Relentless (Dorja)|''Relentless'']]. Aboard the unknown alien ship had been the [[Information broker/Legends|information broker]] [[Talon Karrde]], who had uncovered a datacard deep in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] that revealed the Triumvirate's ruse, and had instantly brought the information to Pellaeon. The Supreme Commander revealed to everyone on the ''Relentless''{{'s}} bridge that "Thrawn" was an impostor, and that Tierce was a mere clone of the [[Grodin Tierce|original Grodin Tierce]] who existed as a failed genetic experiment of Thrawn's. Karrde's associate [[Shada D'ukal]] killed Tierce before the clone could shoot Pellaeon, and Flim—in fact relieved that the ruse was over—followed Pellaeon's order to tell all Imperial ships in the battle to stand down. With the Triumvirate defeated, the Mistryl Shadow Guards disappeared back into the galaxy.<ref name="Vision" />

While the Battles of Bothawui and Yaga Minor had been raging, a complete copy of the [[Caamas Document]] had been discovered on the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] planet [[Nirauan/Legends|Nirauan]] by the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], former [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]]-turned-New Republic-ally [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]], and Skywalker's [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]. With the galactic crisis averted, Pellaeon signed [[Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty|peace accords]] with New Republic [[President of the Senate|President]] [[Ponc Gavrisom]] aboard the ''Chimaera'', and the war was finally brought to an end.<ref name="Vision" /> Over the next six years, a number of unreconstructed Imperials fled to the Remnant, taking a vast amount of wealth with them,<ref name="Ruin">''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide II: Ruin]]''</ref> although some were picked off by the [[Privateer/Legends|privateer]] [[Urias Xhaxin]] while en route to Imperial space.<ref name="Onslaught">''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide I: Onslaught]]''</ref> Within the Remnant's borders, economic development after the treaty was slow, with only a handful of pockets of space having available amenities which were comparable to New Republic goods. Those New Republic goods could be imported cheaply, which crippled Imperial industries, caused some Imperial citizens to live in grinding poverty, and led to both unrest and rioting.<ref name="Ruin" />

Among those hit by the economic hardships were a disgruntled group of Imperials who met on the planet [[Dolis 3]] just after the peace declaration. Hurt by budget cuts and delayed paychecks from Yaga Minor, they rallied under former Moff [[Derran Takkar]] and hatched a plot to exact vengeance upon the New Republic by sabotaging the [[Wedding of Luke Skywalker and Mara Jade|wedding of Skywalker and Jade]].<ref name="Union 1">[[Union 1|''Union'' 1]]</ref> The group traveled to Coruscant and scouted out the site of the upcoming ceremony, but [[Banner Sumptor]] began doubting their mission and saved Skywalker from harm's way during a [[Cantina/Legends|bar]] fight that erupted at the Jedi Master's bachelor party.<ref name="Union 2">[[Union 2|''Union'' 2]]</ref> Former [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Emperor's Royal Guard]] [[Chik Apla]] was swiftly knocked [[Sleep/Legends|unconscious]] by Jade when he attacked her during her [[Clothing/Legends|dress]] fitting,<ref name="Union 3">[[Union 3|''Union'' 3]]</ref> and an attempt to corner dressmaker [[Jari'kyn]] on the day of the nuptials was foiled by Sumptor, who had had a full change of [[Heart/Legends|heart]]. Sumptor then tipped Skywalker off to Takkar's plans, and the wedding's ushers were able to hold back the attacking Imperials and their hired [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gangs]]. Only Takkar successfully slipped through and crashed the ceremony, where he threatened to download a [[Computer virus/Legends|computer virus]] into the New Republic's systems and publicly decried the destruction of Imperial culture. Skywalker talked him out of it, however, and the wedding went on as planned.<ref name="Union 4">[[Union 4|''Union'' 4]]</ref> The marriage was seen by many as a symbolic healing of the longtime rift between Rebels and Imperials.<ref name="Union 1" />

By [[22 ABY/Legends|22 ABY]], Pellaeon and a handful of his trusted advisors had had some limited contact with [[Voss Parck/Legends|Voss Parck]]<ref name="Survivor's Quest">''[[Survivor's Quest]]''</ref> and former Imperial [[Baron of the Empire/Legends|Baron]] [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel]]<ref name="Red Sky Blue Flame">{{GamerCite|7|Red Sky, Blue Flame}}</ref> of the [[Empire of the Hand]], an organization that Skywalker and Jade had encountered on Nirauan.<ref name="Survivor's Quest" /> Parck's Nirauan base was in fact the "[[Hand of Thrawn]]" for which the Triumvirate had been searching, and his earlier efforts to turn his fleet over to the Remnant had been thwarted by Skywalker and Jade, who at the time had feared that the consequent bolstering of the Imperial Military would only have prolonged the war.<ref name="Vision" /> Diplomatic relations between the Remnant and the New Republic remained cordial after the treaty, in no small part due to the efforts of Talon Karrde, who had created an agency to facilitate the exchange of intelligence between the two governments. Suspicions between them were consequently low, even among hardliners,<ref name="Ruin" /> but the Remnant nevertheless kept a wary watch on both the New Republic and on the Unknown Regions.<ref name="NJO Sourcebook" />

===Yuuzhan Vong War===
{{Quote|Understand this: If the Yuuzhan Vong can defeat the New Republic, we cannot stand against them.|Grand Admiral Gilad Pellaeon|<ref name="Ruin" />}}
The Empire adopted an isolationist stance until the New Republic's former [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]], [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], went on a diplomatic mission to the Remnant to seek an alliance against the Yuuzhan Vong shortly after the [[Battle of Dantooine (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Battle of Dantooine]]. Grand Admiral Pellaeon, seeing the threat the Yuuzhan Vong posed to the galaxy, persuaded the Council of Moffs to send a fleet to assist the New Republic.

The Imperials rescued a New Republic task force and aided in the [[Battle of Ithor|eventually futile defense]] of the planet [[Ithor/Legends|Ithor]]. When Ithor was devastated by the Yuuzhan Vong's biological weapons, the Council of Moffs ordered Grand Admiral Pellaeon to return to Imperial space and ready Imperial defenses against a possible attack by the extragalactic aliens. Grudgingly, Pellaeon obeyed. Curiously, Pellaeon did not withdraw the entirety of Imperial forces from Republic space, as a small Imperial task force centered around the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] ''[[Erinnic]]'' continued to assist New Republic forces until the [[Battle of Ord Mantell (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Battle of Ord Mantell]]. It appeared some Imperial elements defended Bilbringi prior to the [[Battle of Fondor]].

As the war between the New Republic and the Yuuzhan Vong continued, the Imperial government and general populace grew increasingly convinced that the Yuuzhan Vong would simply bypass the Remnant. For a number of years, they were correct. Even though Pellaeon personally favored of joining the military campaign against the Yuuzhan Vong, the political leadership of the Empire remained officially neutral. Despite yet another attempt on Leia Organa Solo's part to forge a military alliance between the two states, the Remnant continued to stay out of the fighting. Grand Admiral Pellaeon, however, supplied the New Republic with the Empire's highly classified star charts of the Deep Core regions as a gesture of good faith. The New Republic would later use these state secrets to plan a highly successful trap at [[Ebaq 9]].

Perhaps in retaliation for the Empire's hand in the preparation of the [[Battle of Ebaq]], the Yuuzhan Vong [[Battle of Bastion|launched an invasion]] of the Imperial Remnant. Despite achieving the element of surprise and burning the capital city of the Empire to the ground from orbit, the attack would prove disastrous for the Yuuzhan Vong.

Grand Admiral Pellaeon, although confined to a [[Bacta tank/Legends|bacta tank]] after the initial assault on Bastion, was able to defeat the Yuuzhan Vong at the [[Battle of Borosk (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Battle of Borosk]], forcing the alien invasion force to retreat and abandon its attempts to knock the Imperial Remnant out of the war. Following the victory at Borosk, Grand Admiral Pellaeon was again able to ram an alliance down the collective throat of the Imperial Council of Moffs, despite extreme resistance from old guard Imperial politicians such as Moff [[Kurlen Flennic|Flennic]], who came close to attempting an assassination on the "Old Man of the Empire".

While the Remnant lent its fleets to assisting the Galactic Alliance, Grand Admiral Pellaeon personally commanded a squadron that chased down the surviving vessels from the Yuuzhan Vong fleet that had invaded the Empire. After a string of indecisive victories, Pellaeon routed the Yuuzhan Vong fleet at the [[Battle of Esfandia]].

In the operation to retake the [[Bilbringi system/Legends|Bilbringi]] system, a handful of Imperials took part in the near-disastrous battle. Those who found their way into the system were [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE/D Defender]] pilots from the [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|interdictor]] ''[[Wrack]]'', including the ship's captain, [[Mynar Devis]]. All the Imperials were killed when they attempted to disable a Yuuzhan Vong picket ship. Gilad Pellaeon's reaction to learning of Captain Devis's death led [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] to believe Devis was somehow Pellaeon's son.

The Empire participated in numerous other battles near the end of the war, most notably in the defense of the Galactic Alliance's provisional capital on [[Dac/Legends|Dac]] and the invasion and liberation of occupied [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. In a private ceremony following the surrender of the Yuuzhan Vong, Grand Admiral Pellaeon presented Han and Leia Organa Solo with a piece of art from his own personal collection—''[[Killik Twilight]]'', a painting that Pellaeon had inherited from his mentor, Grand Admiral Thrawn.

===In a Galactic Alliance===
[[File:Ausdehnung_Galaktisches_Imperium_40_NSY.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The Imperial Remnant (red) in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]].]]
By [[35 ABY/Legends|35 ABY]], Pellaeon had retired from his post and the Imperial Remnant had become a member state of the Galactic Alliance, while retaining a degree of autonomy over its own affairs. When Pellaeon was the Supreme Commander of the Galactic Alliance, the Remnant became more and more integrated into the Alliance.<ref name="Pellaeon Databank">{{DB|character|giladpellaeon|Pellaeon, Gilad}}</ref>

After the death of [[Cal Omas]], Pellaeon returned to the Remnant and chose to fight against the Sith Lord [[Darth Caedus]], the grandson of Darth Vader and the Alliance [[Chief of State (Galactic Alliance)|Chief of State]] formerly known as Jacen Solo. This action stood in contrast to the Moff Council, who wished to side with him. Pellaeon recalled [[Natasi Daala|Daala]] and her personal [[Maw Irregular Fleet|fleet]]. Shortly thereafter Pellaeon was assassinated by Jacen Solo's apprentice [[Tahiri Veila]]. After recovering Pellaeon's body, Daala and her faction allied themselves with the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]], while the Moff Council sent in their fleets to fight for Caedus, who they may have seen as a new [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] in the making.

===Democratization===
Following the death of Darth Caedus, and the conclusion of the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] offered the Moffs a peace plan so as to end the war: they would have to accept [[Jagged Fel]] as Head of State so that he could supervise the Moffs. Another clause was delivered by Daala, newly elected [[Chief of State (Galactic Alliance)|Chief of State]] of the Galactic Alliance, that half the Moffs would have to be women. The Moffs accepted as the alternative was to be delivered to the Hapans as prisoners of war after their [[nanovirus]] attack on their Queen Mother's ship, supposedly killing her daughter [[Allana Djo Solo|Allana]].
The Remnant at this time possessed at least one Super Star Destroyer of unknown class, the [[Dominion (Star Dreadnought)|''Dominion'']], and fielded it during the [[Battle of Roche]].

Two years after the end of the war, Fel started to negotiate with Natasi Daala, the new Chief of State of the Galactic Alliance, and Turr Phennir to unite their respective governments in a single galactic power. However, the Moffs, led by [[Drikl Lecersen]], were plotting to undermine Fel until they could amass enough incriminating evidence about his secret alliance with the Jedi against Daala's anti-Jedi policies to destroy him and take control of the Remnant. Lecersen soon joined forces with [[Kuati/Legends|Kuati]] Senator [[Haydnat Treen]] who was planning to reunify the Remnant and the Alliance into a new Galactic Empire. The first step in this process was removing Daala and Fel from the leaderships of both galactic powers.

As the unification talks on Coruscant continued, a galactic crisis erupted when galaxywide slave uprisings, assisted by the abolitionist movement known as the [[Freedom Flight]], begun to overthrow planetary governments and slave organizations. Daala, who feared the political instability that would come from these movements, assigned [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] to put down the uprisings. However, during the repression of the [[Blaudu Sextus revolt]], the tensions between the Jedi and Daala finally came from push to shove: the Jedi assigned to Blaudu Sextus intervened to stop the massacre and the media assumed this meant that the Jedi had decided to support the slave uprisings. The Jedi Council, taking this mistake as a sign from the Force as to what they should do, decided to start assisting the uprisings.

In the meantime, Fel had managed to convince the Moffs to fully join the Galactic Alliance. However, his worry over Daala's management of the crisis, specifically when she threatened him into providing Imperial ships so that she could put down the uprisings, led Fel to pull the Remnant out of the talks. A few days later, Daala was arrested by a [[Plan Delta|coup]] orchestrated by both the Jedi and Lecersen's conspirators. Fel openly proclaimed himself in support of the coup which led the Moff Council to denounce him.

Daala [[Escape from the Armand Isard Correctional Facility|escaped]] from prison with help from [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] and set about taking revenge on those who imprisoned her. She started by gathering her [[Maw Irregular Fleet]] and then by coercing the Moffs into supporting her. Most joined her and formed a fleet to take over the Remnant.

An election was held in 44 ABY that saw Admiral Vitor Reige become the Remnant's Head of State.<ref name="Apocalypse" /> The [[Chiloon Rift/Legends|Chiloon Rift]] [[Nebula/Legends|nebula]] in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] which contained one of the largest and most bountiful [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid fields]] in the galaxy, was located midway between the Imperial Remnant and [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]] by [[45 ABY]].<ref name="Crucible">[[Crucible (novel)|''Crucible'']]</ref>

===Legacy===
{{Quote|I was ''tired'' of Fel playing us against each other! It was time for the Moffs to reassert their authority like in the ''old'' days—back in the Remnant!|Moff Geist of Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|<ref name="Legacy 15">[[Legacy (2006) 15|''Legacy'' (2006) 15]]</ref>}}
Jagged Fel eventually retuned to lead the Remnant, and was able to reclaim considerable territory from the Galactic Alliance, <ref name="TEA" />, who reorganized the Imperial Remnant into a [[Fel dynasty|dynastic monarchy]] known as the [[Fel Empire]],<ref name="Legacy 0.5 1" /> and morphed into its first Emperor.<ref name="TEA" />

In [[130 ABY]], the throne of Fel's grandson, Emperor [[Roan Fel]], was [[Attack on Emperor Fel|usurped]] by the Dark Lord of the Sith, [[Darth Krayt]].<ref name="Legacy 1">[[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]]</ref> His new empire, [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]], was opposed by Fel's [[Empire-in-exile]] in the [[Second Imperial Civil War]].<ref name="Legacy 3">[[Legacy (2006) 3|''Legacy'' (2006) 3]]</ref> Moff [[Geist (Moff)|Geist]] of Krayt's Empire rued being a servant to the Sith and in [[137 ABY]] longed for the days of the Imperial Remnant, which had granted its Moffs considerable power.<ref name="Legacy 15" />

==Organization==
===Government===
{{Main|Head of State (Imperial Remnant)|Council of Moffs/Legends}}
{{Quote|Join the Galactic Alliance? Have you gone mad? You can't believe that any of us would ever agree to this!''"<br />"''I don't need your agreement, Kurlen. When I say that I am advising the council, I am only following a formality. This is the way it will be, because this is the way it has to be. I am simply saving you the need to think it through for yourselves.|Moff [[Kurlen Flennic]] and Supreme Commander Gilad Pellaeon, in 28 ABY|<ref name="The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic I: Remnant">''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic I: Remnant]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Yourfathersdaughter.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Gilad Pellaeon, founder of the Imperial Remnant]]
The Imperial Remnant was a [[oligarchy]] statelet that emerged in [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]] from the former [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] who continued to prosecute the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|war]] against the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] until the two sides signed the [[Pellaeon–Gavrisom Treaty|Bastion Accords]] in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]] and brought the war to a final peaceful end.<ref name="TEA" /> Admiral Pellaeon held the rank of [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander of the Imperial Fleet]],<ref name="Specter" /> and by [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]] had become a [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]].<ref name="Destiny's Way Chapter 12">''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]''</ref> He was the leader of the Remnant despite being technically subordinate to the organization's [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Council of Moffs]].<ref name="NEGTC" /> The Moffs appointed him Supreme Commander, and they had the authority to order the fleet into military action. However, several disastrous offensives against the New Republic that lost large swaths of Imperial territory prompted Pellaeon to tell the council in [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]] that he was finished with obeying their orders. The chastened Moffs accepted his declaration,<ref name="Warfare" /> but their consensus was yet required before Pellaeon could make any large-scale decisions. In 19 ABY, the Supreme Commander addressed each Moff as "Your Excellency,"<ref name="Specter" /> but following the Bastion Accords, he maintained a system of checks and balances that prevented any one Moff from creating disorder.<ref name="NJO Sourcebook">''[[The New Jedi Order Sourcebook]]''</ref> By 28 ABY, Pellaeon was picking and choosing what information he shared with the Council, asserting to outsiders that the Remnant was not run by a committee.<ref name="Destiny's Way Chapter 12" /> In that year, he explicitly told the Moff Council that he considered their consensus to be a mere formality.<ref name="The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic I: Remnant" />

[[File:Moffsonorinda.jpg|thumb|right|350px|Admiral Pellaeon confers with the Moffs on Orinda.]]
When Pellaeon temporarily retired following the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the Council of Moffs led the Remnant in his absence.<ref name="Exile Chapter 16">''[[Legacy of the Force: Exile]]''</ref><ref name="CSWE Pellaeon">{{CSWECite|III|18|Pellaeon, Gilad}}</ref> They again took the Remnant's reins following Pellaeon's [[Second Battle of Fondor|death]] in [[41 ABY]]. Moff [[Drikl Lecersen]] served as their ''de facto'' leader in the latter instance. Later in the year, the Moffs agreed to install a young man named [[Jagged Fel]] as the Remnant's new [[Head of State (Imperial Remnant)|Head of State]], a decision forced upon them by the [[Jedi Coalition]] as an alternative to facing the consequences of war crimes which they had recently committed.<ref name="Invincible" /> The Head of State position became a democratically electable one by the Remnant's citizens [[44 ABY|three years later]] as part of a wave of democratic reforms instituted by Fel. Although Admiral [[Vitor Reige]] won the [[Imperial Election of 44 ABY|election]],<ref name="Apocalypse" /> the Remnant was eventually reorganized into a [[Fel dynasty|dynastic]] [[King/Legends|monarchy]] known as the [[Fel Empire]]<ref name="Legacy 0.5 1" /> with Fel as its first [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]].<ref name="Legacy 33">[[Legacy (2006) 33|''Legacy'' (2006) 33]]</ref>

The Imperial Remnant structure of government was a [[Sector/Legends|sectorial]] [[Governor/Legends|governorship]] system, sat on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Orinda/Legends|Orinda]] from 12 ABY to 17 ABY, after which they relocated to [[Bastion/Legends|Bastion]].<ref name="Warfare" /> In 28 ABY, during the Yuuzhan Vong War, the Remnant joined the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]] in order to combine their strength against the [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]], invading [[Yuuzhan Vong]].<ref name="The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic I: Remnant" /> Pellaeon was integrated into [[Galactic Alliance High Command]] in [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]] during the final stages of the war.<ref name="The Unifying Force">''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''</ref> However, the events of the later [[Second Galactic Civil War]] strained the relationship of the two governments, and the Remnant had broken from the Alliance by 43 ABY.<ref name="Outcast" />

Some aspects of the Old Empire that the Remnant maintained included a strong military and limited venues of public expression.<ref name="CSWE Pellaeon" /> Although the Remnant was a military regime in it early days,<ref name="Destiny's Way Chapter 12" /> but was far more progressive than the Old Empire had been, abolishing both [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]] and anti-[[Alien/Legends|alien]] sentiment. However, the Remnant unobtrusively adhered to the strict tenets of Palpatine's [[New Order/Legends|New Order]] and consequently found itself growing increasingly irrelevant to galactic affairs during the Yuuzhan Vong War.<ref name="CSWE Pellaeon" /> A number of Moffs were killed during the Second Galactic Civil War,<ref name="Revelation">''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]''</ref> and when Jagged Fel was appointed Head of State at the end of the conflict, he negotiated a deal with new Galactic Alliance [[Chief of State (Galactic Alliance)|Chief of State]] [[Natasi Daala]] which stipulated that half of the new Moffs would be female.<ref name="Invincible" /> Traditionally Imperial Moffs such as Lecersen were uncomfortable with the deal.<ref name="Omen">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Omen]]''</ref> Further reforms came in 44 ABY when Jagged Fel called a democratic election; he also charged the [[Imperial News Network (Imperial Remnant)|Imperial News Network]] with reporting on government affairs at every level in order to ensure complete governmental accountability.<ref name="Apocalypse" /> The end of the Second Galactic Civil War also saw the [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] Han Solo suggest that the Moffs atone for their war crimes by setting up a mission to aid poverty-stricken worlds;<ref name="Invincible" /> this idea was later realized as the [[Imperial Mission]], born from Jagged Fel's "[[Victory Without War]]" initiative.<ref name="Legacy 0 17">[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]]</ref> During Fel's leadership, he used "Victory Without War" to expand Imperial territory and influence through peaceful diplomacy.<ref name="TEA" />

===Military===
{{Main|Imperial Military/Legends}}
{{Quote|We still hold eight sectors—over a thousand inhabited systems. We have the Fleet, nearly two hundred Star Destroyers strong. We're still very much a force to be reckoned with.|Captain [[Ardiff]], in 19 ABY|<ref name="Specter" />}}
[[File:Remnantnavy.jpg|thumb|left|300px|The Imperial Navy flies into battle with the Yuuzhan Vong.]]
The Imperial Remnant's state was based on having a strong military<ref name="CSWE Pellaeon" /> and a Supreme [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] as its leader.<ref name="NEGTC" /> The [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]] consisted of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]],<ref name="Specter" /> [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]],<ref name="The Unifying Force" /> and the [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]]. The military was answerable to [[Imperial High Command/Legends|Imperial High Command]]. [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] was used by the Imperial Remnant,<ref name="Specter" /> as were [[Imperial Navy Intelligence|Fleet Intelligence]]<ref name="The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic I: Remnant" /> and [[Communications Intelligence]],<ref name="Vortex Chapter 7">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Vortex]]''</ref> and a [[Ubiqtorate/Legends|Ubiqtorate]] [[Ubiqtorate base|station]] was kept at the planet [[Yaga Minor/Legends|Yaga Minor]].<ref name="Specter" /> [[Rimcee Station]] was a prison complex housing enemies of the Imperial Remnant. Another space station included [[Mulvar Sensor Station]]. Imperial [[Agent/Legends|agents]] were routinely dispatched to worlds outside of [[Imperial Space]],<ref name="Ruin" /> and by the early stages of the Yuuzhan Vong War, they had infiltrated such New Republic operations as the [[New Republic Senate/Legends|Senate]] and various planetary governments.<ref name="NJO Sourcebook" />

Each Moff [[Governor/Legends|governed]] a [[Sector/Legends|sector]] and commanded a [[Sector fleet/Legends|Sector Fleet]]. The various fleets of the [[Navy/Legends|navy]] were largely made up of [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]], 200 of which composed the Imperial Fleet in 19 ABY—Pellaeon lamented the number as being a pale shadow of the 25,000+ arsenal which the Galactic Empire had once boasted.<ref name="Specter" /> Throughout the Remnant's history, its Star [[Destroyer/Legends|Destroyer]] complement included at least [[Megador|three]] [[Dominion (Star Dreadnought)|star]] [[Unidentified Super Star Destroyer|dreadnoughts]], [[Sorannan-class Star Destroyer|''Sorannan''-class Star Destroyers]],<ref name="Warfare" /> [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyers]], [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyers]],<ref name="Specter" /> [[Victory II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory II''-class Star Destroyers]], [[Interdictor-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Interdictor''-class Star Destroyers]],<ref name="The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic I: Remnant" /> [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|''Interdictor''-class heavy cruisers]],<ref name="Warfare" /> [[Turbulent-class Star Destroyer|''Turbulent''-class Star Destroyers]],<ref name="Revelation" />. Also in the fleet were [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]], [[Frigate/Legends|frigates]], [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|''Lambda''-class shuttles]], [[Spacetug/Legends|tugs]], [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruisers]],<ref name="The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic I: Remnant" /> [[Crusader-class corvette|''Crusader''-class corvettes]],<ref name="Message to Spacers 8">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/Mesop8|text=Message to Spacers 8}}</ref> [[Victory II-class frigate|''Victory II''-class frigates]],<ref name="Invasion: Revelations 5">[[Invasion: Revelations 5|''Invasion: Revelations'' 5]]</ref> and [[Strike-class medium cruiser|''Strike''-class medium cruisers]]—for a time, the Remnant depended on the ''Strike''-class more heavily than on even Star Destroyers, as it was one of the few classes of [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] which they had the capacity to produce in large numbers.<ref name="Starships of the Galaxy 135">[[Starships of the Galaxy (Saga Edition)|''Starships of the Galaxy'', Saga Edition]]</ref> As early as 44 ABY, Remnant space was patrolled by [[Burst Fire-class Deep-Space Patrol Vessel|''Burst Fire''-class Deep-Space Patrol Vessels]], ships whose small bays contained boxy military-grade rescue [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttles]].<ref name="Mercy Kill" /> [[Golan space defense platform|Golan Defense Platforms]] were used to guard the Remnant's planets. [[Baradium missile|Baradium warheads]], powerful missiles capable of devastating planets, were used as well.

As Imperial Head of State, Jagged Fel secretly maintained the [[Empire of the Hand]],<ref name="Ascension">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Ascension]]''</ref> a state to which he had once belonged.<ref name="Ruin" /> The Empire fleet included [[Chiss Star Destroyer]]s and [[Nssis-class Clawcraft|''Nssis''-class Clawcraft]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]].<ref name="Ascension" /> Other starfighters employed by the Remnant were [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]],<ref name="Specter" /> [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptors]],<ref name="Vision" /> [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]], [[TIE/sr starfighter]]s, [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE defenders]],<ref name="The Unifying Force" /> and [[starhunter]]s.<ref name="Invincible" /> [[Preybird-class starfighter|''Preybird''-class starfighters]] were briefly utilized by the fleet in 19 ABY through a deal that Moff [[Vilim Disra]] struck with the [[Cavrilhu Pirates]].<ref name="Specter" /> The Imperial [[Army/Legends|army]] included [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transports]], [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|All Terrain Scout Transports]], [[HAVw A5 Juggernaut]]s, [[TIE crawler/Legends|TIE crawlers]], [[HAVr A9 Floating Fortress/Legends|HAVr A9 Floating Fortresses]], and including the [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Assault/Legends|All Terrain Scout Transport/Assaults]]. [[Sentinel-class landing craft/Legends|''Sentinel''-class landing craft]] were used as primary transports.
